2023-12-23.abstract: Javier Milei warned that things would get worse before they got better. Now Argentines are living it.

2023-12-24.title: Freed From Never-Ending Detention, They Ended Up in Another Limbo
2023-12-24.abstract: An Australian high court allowed the release of dozens of detained people who are now subject to curfews and monitored by the government with ankle bracelets.

2023-12-24.title: Israel Relies on Combat Dogs in Gaza Strip
2023-12-24.abstract: The Israeli military says its dogs support elite commando units and save the lives of soldiers. But the canine unit also draws criticism.

2023-12-24.title: He Was Ready to Die, but Not to Surrender
2023-12-24.abstract: How a Ukrainian soldier escaped from the embattled Azovstal steel complex in Mariupol and sneaked 125 miles to Ukrainian territory.

2023-12-24.title: An Abandoned Cemetery Highlights a Painful Colonial Episode for France
2023-12-24.abstract: Family groups have been calling for the excavation of land believed to hold the bodies of at least 50 children — the sons and daughters of Algerians who fought for France — who died in internment camps.

2023-12-24.title: With Israeli Raids in the West Bank, ‘There’s No Such Thing as Sleeping at Night’
2023-12-24.abstract: The Jenin refugee camp in the Israeli-occupied West Bank is a focal point of what Israeli officials describe as counterterrorism operations across the territory.

2023-12-25.title: Israel Says It Is Intensifying Its Campaign Against Hamas
2023-12-25.abstract: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said 15 Israeli soldiers had been killed since Friday, but vowed to keep fighting.

2023-12-25.title: Christmas Moves to Dec. 25 in Ukraine, Another Rebuff of Russia
2023-12-25.abstract: The Ukrainian Orthodox Church formally changed the main date for the festivities, departing from the Russian tradition of celebrating on Jan. 7, according to the Julian calendar.

2023-12-25.title: Hoping for Peace With Houthis, Saudis Keep Low Profile in Red Sea Conflict
2023-12-25.abstract: Riyadh is seeking to avoid getting dragged back into a bloody clash with the Yemeni militia, which has sowed chaos by attacking shipping and firing missiles at Israel.

2023-12-25.title: Israel’s Economy Expected to Shrink 2% as War Sidelines Workers
2023-12-25.abstract: A large segment of the Israeli work force has been called up to fight or become jobless as a result of the conflict with Hamas.

2023-12-25.title: Mongolians Are Circus Stars All Over the World, Except at Home
2023-12-25.abstract: Contortionists and acrobats with celestial skills train in squalid conditions as the promise of a former sumo wrestler to restore the national circus to its past glory has gone mostly unfulfilled.

2023-12-26.title: As World’s Gaze Shifts to Gaza, Israel’s Psyche Remains Defined by Oct. 7 Attack
2023-12-26.abstract: Hamas’s brutal raid and taking of hostages has left Israelis deeply traumatized and is expected to reshape the country for years to come.

2023-12-26.title: Tuesday Briefing
2023-12-26.abstract: Benjamin Netanyahu’s vow to continue the war in Gaza.

2023-12-26.title: Israel’s National Library Reopens After Delay Caused by Hamas Attacks
2023-12-26.abstract: In a traumatized nation, the library has had to rethink its role and adapt its programming.

2023-12-26.title: Nearly Two Million Crowd Into Gaza’s South as Fighting Intensifies
2023-12-26.abstract: As Israel’s ground campaign broadens in southern Gaza, thousands more people are pouring into areas that are struggling to offer shelter or security.

2023-12-26.title: In This Mexican Neighborhood, Locals Say ¡Viva el Beetle!
2023-12-26.abstract: A northern community in Mexico City cannot give up on the famous 1960s hippie-favorite Volkswagen.

2023-12-27.title: Israel Will Not Soon End Its War With Hamas, Officials Say
2023-12-27.abstract: Amid global pressure to reach a cease-fire and fears of a widening regional war, Israel says it will continue to fight in Gaza until Hamas is defeated.

2023-12-27.title: Ukraine’s Stolen Children
2023-12-27.abstract: Thousands of young Ukrainians were separated from their parents by the Russian authorities in the early stages of the war. They are among the most forlorn victims of the invasion.

2023-12-27.title: Lee Sun-kyun, ‘Parasite’ Actor, Found Dead at 48
2023-12-27.abstract: Mr. Lee, a familiar face on Korean television and movie screens, rose to international fame after starring in the Oscar-winning film.

2023-12-27.title: Death by Doctor May Soon Be Available for the Mentally Ill in Canada
2023-12-27.abstract: The country is divided over a law that would allow patients suffering from mental health illnesses to apply for assisted death.

2023-12-27.title: Skepticism Grows Over Israel’s Ability to Dismantle Hamas
2023-12-27.abstract: Israel has vowed time and again to eliminate the group responsible for the brutal Oct. 7 attack, but critics increasingly see that goal as unrealistic or even impossible.

2023-12-28.title: Jacques Delors, Passionate Architect of European Unity, Dies at 98
2023-12-28.abstract: As the executive of the European Union for a decade, he oversaw its increasing economic integration and led the drive for a single currency, the euro.

2023-12-28.title: Israel Warns That Time for Diplomacy With Lebanon Is ‘Running Out’
2023-12-28.abstract: As rockets from Iranian-backed Hezbollah forces streamed in from Lebanon, Israeli officials said they were prepared to take the war to a second front.

2023-12-28.title: A Pint of Wine? Britain Plans to Bring Back an Old-Fashioned Measure.
2023-12-28.abstract: Boris Johnson called the use of imperial measurements “an ancient liberty” and pledged to bring them back after Brexit. The public didn’t want that, so the government settled for pint-size wine.

2023-12-28.title: Foreigners Who Made Ukraine Home Stay Put, Despite War
2023-12-28.abstract: As millions fled, some expatriates made the unlikely decision to remain in Ukraine, among them students determined to finish their education and others who now consider it home.

2023-12-28.title: Criticize This African Country’s Army and You Might Be Drafted
2023-12-28.abstract: The military junta in Burkina Faso, a West African nation struggling to defeat extremist groups, has been forcibly conscripting critics, say human rights organizations.

2023-12-29.title: Israel Expresses Regret for ‘Unintended Harm’ to Civilians in Gaza Airstrike
2023-12-29.abstract: The Israeli military said it was “working to draw lessons” from an attack in central Gaza that was reported to have killed dozens of people.

2023-12-29.title: Hong Kong Activist Flees to Britain, Citing Police Pressure
2023-12-29.abstract: The activist, Tony Chung, was imprisoned after advocating for the territory’s independence. Even after his release, he said, his situation remained oppressive.

2023-12-29.title: In a British Town, a New Way of Caring for Older People Is Bringing Hope
2023-12-29.abstract: An “integrated care center” brings doctors, physiotherapists, social workers and pharmacists under one roof. It won’t solve Britain’s underlying social care crisis — but it could help.

2023-12-29.title: Pilot Pulled the Wrong Levers in Nepal Crash That Killed 72, Investigators Find
2023-12-29.abstract: One pilot changed the propeller angle instead of that of the wing flaps while trying to land a Yeti Airlines plane in January, a report said.

2023-12-29.title: Russia Pounds Ukrainian Cities in One of the Largest Air Attacks of the War
2023-12-29.abstract: The missile and drone attacks killed at least 30 people and damaged critical industrial and military infrastructure, part of a wintertime campaign that Ukraine had been dreading.

2023-12-30.title: Where Was the Israeli Military?
2023-12-30.abstract: A Times investigation found that troops were disorganized, out of position and relied on social media to choose targets. Behind the failure: Israel had no battle plan for a massive Hamas invasion.

2023-12-30.title: How a Super Affordable Bakery Chain Became a British Culinary Icon
2023-12-30.abstract: A festive pop-up restaurant is the latest crowd-pleasing stunt from Greggs, a mass-market bakery that is riding out Britain’s cost-of-living crisis with gusto.

2023-12-30.title: As War Rages in Ukraine, Denmark Turns an Office Park Back Into an Arsenal
2023-12-30.abstract: The conflict and surging arms production in Russia have spurred demand for ammunition manufacturing across Europe.

2023-12-30.title: Italy’s Raucous Holiday Classics Are Not Your Standard Hallmark Movies
2023-12-30.abstract: Gleefully vulgar, the genre known as “Cinepanettone” once dominated Italy over the holidays. Italian revelers recently flocked to the alpine town where many of the films were set.

2023-12-30.title: When a Restaurant Is More Than a Place to Dine
2023-12-30.abstract: We asked Canada Letter readers to tell us about the restaurants that have become institutions in their communities.

2023-12-31.title: Defying U.S. Pressure, Israel Deepens Gaza Assault
2023-12-31.abstract: Under the withering attacks, 165 people were killed in the past 24 hours, Gazan officials said, while Israel said it had destroyed the apartment of a Hamas leader.

2023-12-31.title: The World in Stories: 13 Favorite Dispatches From 2023
2023-12-31.abstract: Our correspondents ventured to some of the world’s most remote, and dangerous, locales to report stories that reveal a country’s culture and the human condition. Here are our favorites from the year.

2023-12-31.title: Ukraine, Stalled on the Front, Steps Up Sabotage, Targeting Trains
2023-12-31.abstract: As conventional forces struggle to break through defensive lines, both sides are increasingly turning to guerrilla tactics.

2023-12-31.title: ‘You Carry Your Home With You’: A Palestinian Poet’s Journey Out of Gaza
2023-12-31.abstract: After months of war and fleeing from one place to the next with family, Palestinian Poet Mosab Abu Toha crossed into Egypt. He describes his harrowing experience, including being stripped, beaten and detained by Israeli soldiers.

2023-12-31.title: At These Schools, Arab and Jewish Students Share Their Feelings, With Each Other
2023-12-31.abstract: At a time when support for peace is at a low, conversations at Israel’s Hand in Hand schools about the Oct. 7 attack and the war in Gaza sound different than they do elsewhere.
